kw.name: Connect To Database
kw.msg: Connect to DB using : pymysql.connect(db='rpacourse', user='robot', passwd=***, host='localhost', port=3306, charset='utf8mb4')
kw.doc: Creates a database connection using the DB API 2.0 ``db_module`` and the parameters provided. Along with listed commonly used arguments (`db_name`, `db_host` etc.) you can set any other DB module specific parameters as key/value pairs.

kw.name: Query
kw.msg:
Executing sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM invoicestatus;

Query returned 1 rows
RowCOUNT(*)
00
kw.doc: Runs a query with the ``select_statement`` and returns the result as list of rows. The type of row values depends on the database module - usually they are tuples or tuple-like objects.

kw.doc: Catenates the given items together and returns the resulted string.

kw.name: Execute Sql String
kw.msg:
Executing sql:
insert into invoiceheader(invoicenumber, companyname, companycode, referencenumber,invoicedate, duedate, bankaccountnumber,amountexclvat, vat, totalamount, invoicestatus_id, comments)values('1543235', 'Component Oy', '1234567-9', '15432359','2022-10-01', '2022-10-15', 'FI05 1234 5600 7891 01','465', '111.6', '576.6','1', 'Reference number error');
kw.doc: Executes the ``sql_string`` as a single SQL command.
